name: Light Swiss Enchiladas
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Low fat Swiss cheese grated
recipeInstructions:
Servings: 4
Can use reduced-fat Swiss or Jack cheese, grated, 2 cups. Omit salt if stock is already salted.
Preheat oven to 350°F.
Wrap tortillas tightly in foil and place in the preheated oven for 15 minutes to soften. While tortillas are warming, cook onion and garlic, covered, over low heat until soft. Add a little water or stock if necessary to prevent burning. Add chicken and cook over medium heat, stirring frequently until cooked, about 5 minutes. Add tomato puree, chiles, and salt; mix well. Simmer uncovered for 10 minutes.
While the chicken mixture is simmering, combine stock and dry milk powder in a saucepan and stir thoroughly. Slowly heat to simmer, stirring frequently. Dip each tortilla in the milk sauce. Divide chicken filling, spooning about 1/4 cup of the mixture down the center of each tortilla. Roll each tortilla around the filling, seam side down, and place them in a greased glass baking dish with a non-stick vegetable coating. Pour milk sauce over top and bake at 350°F for 20 minutes. Remove from oven and sprinkle grated cheese evenly over top. Return to oven for another 5 minutes until cheese is melted.
To serve, place each enchilada on a plate and garnish with fresh cilantro if desired. This recipe may also be made in individual au gratin dishes. Calories per enchilada: 200; Cholesterol: 25 mg; Fat: 4 Grams; Sodium: 278 mg
